title: Winding Number As The Degree Of S2 To S2
summary: The topological charge of an isolated bad point is the degree of the map from a small enclosing sphere in momentum space to the target sphere of normalized b-vectors.
assumptions: The enclosing sphere does not intersect any other bad point.
regime: Topological classification of isolated point nodes.
scope: Lecture One degree-theory formulation of Weyl-node charge.

source_anchors.notes: The integer is presented both as an integral density and as the pullback degree.

representation: Integral formula for the degree of the normalized b-map.

math_expressions.label: wnn
math_expressions.context: Degree-theory form of the winding number.

math_expressions.label: weta
math_expressions.context: Normalization and closure of the target-sphere volume form.

symbols.meaning: winding number on the enclosing sphere

failure_modes: If the sphere encloses multiple nodes, the integral returns the total charge rather than a single-node charge.
retrieval_hints: Use for topological-charge calculations around a Weyl node.
